title: Joining EY for the Summer
excerpt: I'll be spending this summer as a consulting intern at EY. A short note on why this seat, why now, and what I'm hoping to learn before I'm back at a desk in the fall.

title: Concentration Risk Isn't Going Anywhere
excerpt: A handful of names still drive most of the S&P's variance. The question isn't whether that's healthy — it's how to think about owning the index when it isn't.

title: A Framework for Reading Central Bank Press Conferences
excerpt: Most of what's said is choreography. The signal is in what they chose not to say — and where they paused.

title: The Carry Trade Doesn't Care About Your Thesis
excerpt: Why FX carry keeps working long after the academic argument says it shouldn't, and what that tells you about market structure.

title: Notes from the Earnings Tape
excerpt: A working set of observations from this quarter's prints — what beat, what missed, and what the price action actually said.

title: Why I'm Skeptical of the Soft Landing
excerpt: The yield curve has dis-inverted, unemployment is creeping up, and the consensus has quietly migrated from 'no landing' to 'soft landing.' That migration itself is the tell.

title: What This Is, and Why
excerpt: The bottom line is what's left when the noise clears. This is a working view on markets and economies — short pieces on what's moving the price, what the data says, and what the consensus is getting wrong.
